customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high compliance. Customer is a first-time solar inquirer who has never engaged with the category before — no prior bad experiences, no competitor framing, no skepticism signals. Primary posture is transactional: willing to proceed but not emotionally invested. No enthusiasm was built on the call, so the closer should not assume warmth.
unresolved_concerns: Cash-purchase path: customer stated intent to pay cash when the credit qualifier was raised. Rep acknowledged but did not confirm whether Ion's cash option is available, what it includes, or how it differs from the financed path. Closer must address this proactively.
No bill anchor established: customer is pre-move-in and has no bill for this address. Closer has no bill pain to work from. Will need to establish usage baseline and cost context from scratch.

No emotional motivation articulated: customer's interest in solar was never developed beyond 'I was trying to get it before I get there.' Closer should open by drawing out the customer's own motivation before presenting the design.
sensitive_topics_raised: Customer is pre-move-in — has not yet taken possession of the home. Closer should confirm move-in has occurred or is imminent before proceeding with the proposal.
exact_promises_rep_made: Everything will be sent via email while on the phone.
Call will be by phone (not in-person).
Customer should not be driving during the call.
closer_should_know: This is a cash buyer — the customer explicitly said 'cash price deal' when the credit qualifier came up. The closer must come prepared with Ion's cash-purchase option and pricing structure. Walking in with a financed-system proposal will create an immediate mismatch.
